app.consoleLine2: Open DevTools (F12) and use Inspector to find credentials!

selector.title: 🛡️ JS - FRONT-END HACKERGAME
selector.subtitle: Progressive Security Challenge - Find Username & Password
selector.tip2: Press F12 → Inspector tab to inspect HTML
selector.tip3: Use Console tab to execute JavaScript commands

challenge.devtoolsHelp: 💻 Use DevTools (F12) to inspect the page and find the credentials

1.title: Level 1: Plain Sight
1.description: The credentials are hidden in plain sight. Check the Inspector!
1.hint: Look at the HTML source. The answer is written in black text on black background.

2.title: Level 2: CSS Hidden
2.description: Use DevTools Inspector to uncover hidden styles.
2.hint: The password might be hidden with CSS display: none or opacity: 0

3.title: Level 3: DOM Manipulation
3.description: The JavaScript console has been used to hide the credentials.
3.hint: Check the Console tab - look for logged messages or variables.
3.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in a JavaScript variable and logged to the console. Find the variable name and its value in the Console!

4.title: Level 4: HTML Attributes
4.description: Data is stored in HTML attributes. Inspect the elements!
4.hint: Look for data-* attributes or hidden input fields.
4.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in HTML attributes, such as data-username and data-password. Use the Inspector to find these attributes and read their values!

5.title: Level 5: Base64 Encoding
5.description: The credentials are encoded. Use the Console to decode.
5.hint: Try using atob() function in the console: atob("encoded_string")
5.showInfo: Base64 encoding is a common method to hide credentials. Use the atob() function in the console to decode them.

6.title: Level 6: CSS Injection
6.description: Find the password hidden in the CSS file.
6.hint: Check the Network tab for CSS files, or look for inline styles with unusual content.
6.showInfo: In this level, the password is hidden in a CSS file. Use the DevTools Network tab to find the CSS file, then search for the password inside it!

7.title: Level 7: JavaScript Minified
7.hint: Check the Console or beautify the JavaScript. Look for string concatenation.
7.hint2: In this level, the password is hidden in a minified JavaScript file. Use DevTools to open the file, then use the 'Pretty Print' function to make the code readable. Look for the password among the variables!
7.showInfo: Minified JavaScript files are hard to read, but you can format them nicely with DevTools 'Pretty Print' function. In this level, the password is hidden in such a file and given by string concatenation. Find the variables that are concatenated and try to reconstruct the password!

8.title: Level 8: Local Storage Hunt
8.description: Check the Application/Storage tab for LocalStorage secrets.
8.hint: Right-click → Inspect → Application tab → LocalStorage. Look for stored values.
8.showInfo: LocalStorage is a common place to store credentials. Use the DevTools Application tab to view LocalStorage content and find the credentials!

9.title: Level 9: Network Request
9.description: The credentials are fetched via XHR in the background.
9.hint: Check the Network tab when the page loads. Look for API calls or data endpoints.
9.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in a network request. Use the DevTools Network tab to view all network requests and find the one containing the credentials!

10.title: Level 10: Cookie Extraction
10.description: Look for credentials hidden in cookies or sessionStorage.
10.hint: Console → document.cookie or check Application tab → Cookies.
10.showInfo: Cookies and sessionStorage are common places to store credentials. Use the Console to view document.cookie or the Application tab to view Cookies, and find the credentials!

11.title: Level 11: Regex Pattern Matching
11.description: The credentials are hidden in regex patterns. Decode them!
11.hint: Open Console and check for regex patterns. Try evaluating them or looking in global variables.
11.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in a regex pattern. Use the Console to find the regex pattern, then try to decode the credentials based on the pattern!

12.title: Level 12: Chained Base64
12.description: Multiple layers of Base64 encoding. Decode recursively!
12.hint: Use atob() multiple times. Try: atob(atob(atob(encoded_var)))
12.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden with multiple layers of Base64 encoding. Use the atob() function multiple times in the console to recursively decode the credentials!

13.title: Level 13: CSS Custom Properties
13.description: Credentials stored in CSS custom properties (variables).
13.hint: Inspect element styles. Look for --username and --password CSS variables. Get them via getComputedStyle().
13.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in CSS custom properties (variables). Use the Inspector to find these variables, then use the getComputedStyle() function in the console to read their values!

14.title: Level 14: IndexedDB Storage
14.description: Credentials stored in IndexedDB database. Check Application tab!
14.hint: DevTools → Application → IndexedDB. Open the 'credentials' database and find the data.
14.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in an IndexedDB database. Use the DevTools Application tab to view IndexedDB content and find the 'credentials' database where the credentials are stored!

15.title: Level 15: WebWorker Messages
15.description: Credentials transmitted via Web Worker. Intercept the message!
15.hint: Check Console for worker messages. The credentials are passed to a Worker and logged back.
15.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in a Web Worker message. Use the Console to view worker messages and find the one containing the credentials!

16.title: Level 16: Hexadecimal Encoding
16.description: Credentials encoded in hexadecimal. Decode them!
16.hint: Convert hex strings to ASCII. Try: String.fromCharCode(...).
16.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden with hexadecimal encoding. Use functions like String.fromCharCode() in the console to convert hex strings to ASCII characters and find the credentials!

17.title: Level 17: Binary Data
17.description: Credentials stored as binary data in an ArrayBuffer.
17.hint: Use the Console to read the ArrayBuffer. Try: new Uint8Array(window.BINARY_DATA).
17.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in an ArrayBuffer as binary data. Use the Console to read the ArrayBuffer, for example with new Uint8Array(), to find the credentials!

18.title: Level 18: Shadow DOM Secrets
18.description: Credentials hidden in Shadow DOM. Inspect the DOM tree!
18.hint: Inspector shows Shadow DOM. Look for #shadow-root in the DOM tree.
18.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are hidden in the Shadow DOM. Use the Inspector to view the DOM tree and find the #shadow-root element where the credentials are stored!

19.title: Level 19: Mixed Cipher
19.description: Credentials encoded with multiple layers: Base64 + ROT13 + Hex.
19.hint: Decode step by step: Hex → Base64 → ROT13. Use online tools or JS functions.
19.showInfo: In this level, the credentials are encoded with multiple layers: first Base64, then ROT13, finally Hex. Decode step by step to find the credentials!
19.consoleLog: Encoded username and password stored. Decode them!

20.title: Level 20: ULTIMATE CHALLENGE
20.description: All techniques combined. Full obfuscation and multiple encoding layers.
20.hint: Use ALL your skills: Inspector, Console, Network, decode multiple formats, check all storage types.
20.showInfo: In this final level, all previous techniques are combined: the credentials are hidden with multiple layers of encoding and stored in various places. Use all the skills you've learned to crack this challenge!
